title: 50bb UTG RFI
notes: 50bb 6-max UTG open, 2.2bb, callPct=0 (RFI). Pairs 55+ pure, 22-44 mixed. Suited: broadways pure, Ax down to A6s + wheel A5s/A4s emphasized (blocker value, A5s>A4s>A3s>A2s monotonic), suited connectors thinned 54s+. Offsuit kept tight (tighter than 100bb): AJo+ strong, ATo/KJo/QJo partial only, KTo cut entirely, no weak Kxo/Qxo. Monotonic by hand strength within each family. Approximate study-grade, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb MP RFI
notes: MP (UTG+1/LJ) RFI at 50bb, 2.2bb, raise-or-fold so callPct=0 everywhere. Corrected weighted width ~18.9% (~250 combos), aligned to published solver charts for an ~18% MP open. Structure: all pairs 44+ pure-open, 33 mostly, 22 partial. Suited broadways AKs-ATs/KQs-KTs/QJs-QTs/JTs/T9s/98s/87s/76s pure or near-pure. Wheel aces A5s pure, A4s-A2s descending mix; Ax-mid (A8s-A6s) trimmed since MP has no rake-busting incentive of late position. Offsuit ends at ATo/KTo/QTo/JTo partial. Suited connectors run 98s-54s at declining freq; one-gappers and weak Kx/Qx suited mostly folded. Frequencies approximate for study, not exact.

title: 50bb CO RFI
notes: Approximate study-grade CO RFI (~26%) aligned with published solver charts, not exact. 2.2bb open, no flat-call option (callPct=0). CO opens ALL pocket pairs pure (22-44 corrected from partials to 100% — small pairs are pure opens from CO). All suited aces A2s-AKs pure. Offsuit aces extend through A9o pure, A8o ~90%, with A7o/A6o/A5o/A4o as declining partials. Kxs runs pure down to K6s with K5s-K2s tapering. Kxo: KTo pure, K9o ~60% partial. Q/J broadway offsuit (QJo/QTo) pure; Q9o/J9o/T9o/98o partials. Suited connectors strong down to 76s pure, with 54s/65s near-pure and gappers (53s/64s/75s/86s/97s) as partials. Bottom of range = small/mixed: suited Kx/Qx wheels, weak suited gappers, offsuit Ax bluffs.

title: 50bb BTN RFI
notes: Corrected study-grade BTN RFI at 50bb, ~44% (about 578 combos, 43.6% of 1326). All pairs, all suited aces, all suited kings, and suited queens to Q5s open 100%. Offsuit aces open down to A2o (full above A8o, then descending mix A7o-A2o). Offsuit broadways KQo-K9o, QJo-Q9o, JTo-J9o, T9o open at/near 100%; weakest offsuit (K8o-K7o, Q8o, J8o, T8o, 98o, 97o) are partial. Suited connectors/gappers extend to 32s at low frequency. callPct=0 since BTN is first-in and cannot flat. Frequencies are illustrative, not exact solver output. Lowest-EV hands (32s, 74s, J5s, Q2s, A2o) drop first if opening tighter.

title: 50bb SB RFI (raise-or-fold)
notes: SB RFI is a pure raise-or-fold node (no limping in this config), so callPct=0 and non-fold frequency = aggroPct for every hand. Weighted opening frequency is ~38-39% of combos, consistent with the stated ~40% target for a 50bb SB facing only the BB. All pairs, all suited aces, all suited broadways, and strong suited connectors are pure or near-pure opens. Offsuit aces, weak offsuit broadways, weak suited kings/queens/jacks, and low suited connectors/gappers are mixed partial opens that taper monotonically. Wheel suited aces (A2s-A5s) remain pure opens for nut-blocker and postflop equity; wheel OFFSUIT aces get only a small blocker nudge, not a large one. Approximate study-grade chart aligned to published 50bb solver output, not exact.

title: 50bb BB vs BTN
rangePct: ~6.5% polar 3bet (layered on a very wide overall flatting defense)
notes: BB closes the action vs a single BTN raise and gets a great price, so the defend is split between a polar 3bet and a very wide pure-call region — almost nothing in the playable set pure-folds (BB has the best price of any defender). Value head: AA-QQ near-pure 3bet, JJ/TT and AKs/AKo/AQs mixed. Bluff/semi-bluff region is built from suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s, preferred for ace blocker + nut/wheel potential), suited broadways (KQs/KJs/QJs/QTs/JTs/KTs), suited K-blocker hands (K4s-K6s), and suited connectors (T9s-43s). Offsuit broadways (AQo, KQo, AJo, KJo) mix in lightly. Note the per-hand frequencies are modest, so a polar 3bet of THIS construction is ~6-7% of all hands, not 14% — see issuesFound.

title: 50bb BB vs UTG
notes: BB closes the action vs a UTG open, so it defends very wide by calling (great pot odds plus the discounted blind already invested). The 3bet portion is held near ~9% and value-heavy because UTG opens a strong, tight range that continues/4bets well, so bluffs need strong blockers and playability. Value 3bets: QQ+, AKs, AKo, with AQs and JJ at a mix. Bluff 3bets cluster on suited wheel aces (A5s-A3s) for ace blockers and nut potential, plus a sprinkle of suited Broadways (AJs, KQs) and a few suited connectors (98s-65s). At 50bb the flatting range is trimmed slightly versus 100bb because reduced implied odds hurt set-mining and speculative suited hands, so the tail (K9s, 76s, 65s) folds more often. Offsuit Broadways (AJo, KQo) defend mostly by calling rather than 3betting and fold their weakest combos. Medium pairs and Broadways (TT-77, AJs, KQs, JTs) prefer to flat in position-disadvantaged but capped-opponent pots and realize equity.

title: 50bb BB vs MP
rangePct: ~9-10% 3bet (plus a wide ~21% flat-call range on top)
notes: BB closes the action vs a single MP open getting a great price, so the strategy is heavily call-weighted with a polarized ~9-10% 3bet built on top. Value 3bets are pure/near-pure with QQ+ and AK; AQs/JJ/TT/AQo are the high-frequency mixed-value region. Bluff 3bets favor suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s) for the A-blocker plus nut-flush/wheel playability, the worst suited Broadways/Kx (KTs-K5s, QJs-Q9s) and the best suited connectors (87s/76s/65s/54s) that have equity and blockers but are mediocre as pure flats. Everything with reasonable equity at this price defends rather than folding; only the trashiest offsuit and weak suited gappers fold outright. Frequencies are approximate, not solver-exact.

title: 50bb BB vs CO
rangePct: ~11% 3bet (plus a wide ~20% flatting range; ~30-31% total defense)
notes: BB vs CO is the widest 3bet/defense node short of blinds-on-blinds. BB closes the action and has a price, so the flatting range is enormous (broadways, suited gappers, small pairs flat). The 3bet is a mixed linear/polar structure at 50bb: value top (QQ+, AK near-pure, AQs/AQo/AJs/JJ/TT mixing) blended with high-frequency blocker bluffs. Suited wheel aces A5s-A2s are the premium bluffs (nut-flush + wheel blockers) and 3bet near or above 50%. Kxs blockers (K2s-K9s), offsuit Ax (A5o-A2o), and suited connectors/one-gappers fill the rest of the bluff region. Out of position prefer the larger ~11bb size to deny realization. Frequencies are approximate study-grade, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb BB vs SB
notes: BB vs SB single-raised pot, 50bb, BB OOP but closes action vs one very wide opener (~45%). Construction is polar: value region (QQ+, AK, AQs, JJ/TT, KQs) is now near-PURE 3bet because flatting premiums OOP just lets the SB realize equity in position; bluffs are low-equity hands that flat poorly and block continues (A5s-A2s wheel aces heaviest, A7s-A6s nut-blockers, Ax-offsuit blockers, K-low suited blockers K5s-K2s, plus suited connectors/gappers as semi-bluff 3bets). Everything else flats wide. ~14.5% combo-weighted 3bet, ~26% call, ~41% total defend. Frequencies are approximate study aids and shift with the SB's actual open size/frequency; a larger 10-12bb size would trim the bluff portion.

title: 50bb SB vs BTN
notes: SB vs BTN single-raised pot, 50bb. SB plays near 3bet-or-fold OOP: flatting with the BB left to act and no closing action is unattractive, so callPct=0 throughout by construction. Range is polar: linear value (TT+, AQs+, AJs+, AKo/AQo, KQ, suited broadways) plus bluffs concentrated in wheel suited aces (A5s-A2s, blocker + nut-flush playability), A6s-A8s, low K-high suited, and suited connectors/one-gappers. Wheel aces favored as bluffs for ace+5 blockers. Offsuit broadways thin quickly OOP; offsuit wheel aces (A5o/A4o) kept at a token 10% blocker frequency only. Small pairs are weak 3bet bluffs OOP (poor blockers, bad equity-when-called) so they taper sharply (44=15, 33/22=10). aggroPct reflects mixed solver frequencies; remainder folds. Sizing ~10bb (~4x a 2.5bb open) is standard OOP pressure at 50bb. Study-grade approximation, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb BTN vs CO
notes: BTN vs CO is the widest IP cold-call spot, so this is a flat-heavy strategy (~14% flat) with a polarized ~8-9% 3bet on top. Value 3bets at 50bb: AA-QQ, AKs/AKo, AQs, plus mixed JJ/AJs/KQs. Bluffs lean on suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s, the premium A5s/A4s blockers), K-high suited blockers (K5s-K4s), and the better suited connectors. Offsuit aces below AJo are mostly pure folds IP because they realize poorly and are dominated by CO's continuing range. JJ/TT/AQo are largely mixed flats given strong position and a wide CO opening range. Small pairs flat freely for set value (only marginal 22-44 mix any folds at 50bb). Approximate study-grade frequencies, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb CO vs UTG
notes: Value-heavy, low-bluff 3bet vs a tight UTG open at 50bb. Pure value core: AA-KK and AKs at 100%; QQ/AKo nearly pure (small flat slice). AQs is the marginal value/mix at ~55%. JJ/TT/99 flat the large majority at 50bb since CO has position and a strong calling alternative exists vs UTG's narrow range. Primary bluffs are A5s/A4s (nut-flush ace blockers, best blocker+playability), with A3s a thinner blocker bluff. Suited Broadways (AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs) and T9s mostly flat with a small 3bet slice. Offsuit hands (AQo, KQo) are mostly fold with tiny mix; AQo flats some but is a clear fold-heavy hand vs UTG. Versus a UTG 4bet, continue with QQ+/AK and drop the bluffs except occasional A5s as a 5bet-jam blocker. Frequencies are study-grade approximations, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb SB vs CO
notes: SB vs CO is pure 3bet-or-fold (no flat) OOP with BB still to act, so all callPct=0 and every listed hand splits cleanly aggro/fold. Structure is polar: a value/merge top (TT+, AK/AQ/AJ, KQs, ATs, AJs) plus a bluff region built from low suited-ace blockers (A2s-A5s, weighted toward the wheel aces) and suited broadways/connectors (KTs, QJs, JTs, T9s, 98s, 87s, 76s, suited gappers). Final width ~10.7% of combos, matching the stated ~11%.

title: 50bb BTN vs SB 3bet
rangePct: ~4-5% (4bet; value + A5s/A4s) — NOT 8%; separate flat band 88-JJ/AQs/AJs/KQs
notes: BTN is IP vs the SB 3bet, so the strategy is 4bet-or-fold with a thin in-position flat band. Value 4bets: AA/KK pure, QQ near-pure, AKs/AKo mostly. Bluff 4bets are wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s) that block AA/AKs/AKo and retain equity/playability; they are 4bet-or-fold (no call mix). Flatting band (call, not 4bet): 88-JJ, AQs, AJs, KQs — these realize equity well in position and are NOT 4bet here. AKo never folds to a single 3bet at 50bb; it 4bets or, occasionally, calls. Facing a 5bet, fold the A5s/A4s bluffs and continue only AA/KK/QQ/AKs.

title: 50bb BTN vs BB 3bet
rangePct: ~3.3% 4bet of all hands (value-heavy, ~27% bluffs); call ~11-12% medium-strong; rest fold. NOTE: stated "~7%" was an overstatement — a true 7% (~93 combos) forces unsound pure 4bets of JJ/AQs/KQs and large offsuit bluff blocks; corrected to the realistic ~3.3-4% value-heavy construction.
notes: BTN vs a polarized BB 3bet uses a value-heavy 4bet (~3.3% of all hands, ~27% bluffs) plus a wide disciplined flat in position. Value: AA pure, KK ~98%, QQ ~78%, AKs ~85%, AKo ~72%, AQs/JJ a small mix. Bluffs are nut-blocker Ax-suited wheels (A5s/A4s/A3s/A2s heaviest because they block AA/AK and flop nut-flush/wheel equity) plus a few Kxs (K5s/K4s) that block KK/AK with low call value. Strong-but-dominated hands (JJ-TT, AQs, KQs, suited broadways) flat to keep BB's bluffs in and avoid getting 5bet off equity. In position, BTN calls wide: all pairs down to 22 mix in, suited broadways, suited aces, and suited connectors that realize equity vs an OOP 3bettor. Study-grade approximations, not exact solver output.

title: 50bb CO vs BTN 3bet
notes: Approximate study-grade range, NOT exact solver output. CO is OOP vs BTN's 3bet, so the value 4bet range is condensed: AA/KK pure value, QQ/AKs heavily mixed, AKo mixed. Bluff 4bets favor suited wheel aces (A5s-A3s) for ace-blocker value plus board playability; these mix 4bet/call/fold, weighted toward the better blockers (A5s>A4s>A3s). Most broadways and pocket pairs (AQs, AJs, ATs, KQs, KJs, JJ, TT, 99, 88) prefer flatting given the positional disadvantage rather than 4betting thin. The flat-call range is wider than the 4bet range here precisely because OOP wants to keep its range elastic and avoid getting jammed on. Total 4bet frequency lands near ~6%. Sizing to ~21bb is roughly 2.2x the 3bet and commits a large stack fraction at 50bb, so value 4bets are happy to stack off and bluff 4bets fold to a jam.

title: 50bb SB vs BB 3bet
rangePct: ~8% of opening range 4bets (value core + wheel-Ax suited blockers as bluffs); remainder calls or folds
notes: Polarized/blended 4bet OOP. Value core: AA/KK near-pure 4bet; QQ and AKs/AKo majority 4bet for value/protection (OOP wants to deny equity rather than flat passively). JJ/TT/AQs/AJs/ATs lean heavily toward calling since flatting OOP keeps weaker dominated hands in BB's wide 3bet range. Bluffs are wheel Ax-suited (A5s-A2s) that block AA/AKs and have nut-flush/wheel backup if called; these low-Ax play near-binary 4bet-or-fold (poor flat playability OOP), so call% is small. A few suited Broadways/connectors (KQs, KJs, JTs) rotate in as light blockers but are predominantly flats. Against tight/value-heavy 3bettors, drop the wheel-Ax bluffs and 4bet a linear value range (collapse toward QQ+/AK). Frequencies are approximate study aids, not exact solver outputs; 4bet/call splits shift with sizing and opponent 3bet frequency.

title: FR UTG RFI
notes: Very tight UTG open-or-fold range from 8 players behind in 9-max full-ring. Pure raises (~100% aggro); callPct=0 across the board because UTG opens the pot and there is no prior action to flat-call. Core value (88+, broadway suited aces, AKo/AQo) plus disciplined suited extensions. A5s is the lone wheel-ace blocker/playability hand; lower suited aces (A4s-A2s) remain folds at this tightness, as do A9s, ATo, and offsuit broadways below AQo (KQo/AJo are folds here). Sits at the upper end of published study-grade FR UTG charts at 100bb.

title: FR UTG+1 RFI
notes: RFI (raise-first-in) range for UTG+1 in 9-max with 7 players left to act; callPct=0 throughout since pot is unopened. Structure: pairs 55+ pure opens, 44 high-freq, 33/22 low-freq mix. Suited aces full to ATs then a clean monotonic descent A9s>A8s>A7s; wheel aces preferred for blocker/playability with A5s pure and A4s>A3s>A2s. Offsuit broadways AKo/AQo/AJo pure, ATo mixed, KQo near-pure, KJo low mix. Suited broadways KQs-KTs/QJs-QTs/JTs/T9s pure; K9s/Q9s/J9s/T8s are low-freq trims. Suited connectors descend 98s>87s>76s. Tight by position; speculative offsuit and weak suited hands fold. Use 2.3bb. Study-grade approximation aligned with published solver charts, not exact solver output.

title: FR UTG+2 RFI
notes: FR 9-max UTG+2/MP1 with 6 players still to act is an early-middle position: raise-or-fold, callPct=0 throughout. Value core (AA-66, AKs-ATs, AKo-AJo, KQs-KTs, KQo, QJs/QTs, JTs, A5s) opens at full frequency. Small pairs and offsuit/suited fillers taper to keep the range value-weighted. Offsuit broadways are deliberately restricted (12 combos each) — only AKo/AQo full, AJo high, KQo high, with ATo/KJo/QJo as light mixers — because offsuit combos inflate width fastest from EP. A5s/A4s retained over A6s/A7s for nut-blocker/wheel playability. Width is ~14-15% (weighted), a defensible study-grade FR MP1 size; the original draft's nominal label said ~12% but the listed hands actually summed to ~21.5%, so the label was kept honest to the corrected (tighter) construction.

title: FR LJ RFI
notes: FR 9-max Lojack open, 5 players left to act, so the range is still fairly tight at roughly 15 percent. Pure raise-or-fold strategy (callPct=0); listed aggroPct values are raise frequencies and aggro+fold sums to 100 on every hand. Core: 55+ pure raise, 44-22 partial; all suited Broadways and AJo+/KQo pure; ATo/KJo/QJo are partial-frequency offsuit Broadways. Suited aces extend to A2s with the wheel cards (A5s/A4s) weighted up as blockers/equity and the rest (A8s-A6s, A3s-A2s) as partial bluffs. Suited connectors 98s/87s/76s/65s and one-gappers (T8s, J9s, K9s, Q9s) come in at partial frequency as position improves over UTG/UTG+1. Tighten toward the low end vs aggressive 3-bettors behind, loosen slightly vs passive tables.

title: FR HJ RFI
notes: Hijack opening range for 9-max FR, ~16% of hands, 2.3bb. callPct=0 throughout (unopened RFI, no flat option). Pure-raise core: 66+ all pairs, broadway suited/offsuit, suited aces A2s+ (A5s/A4s pure as wheel-blocker/playability hands), suited connectors 54s+. Mixed-frequency marginals: small pairs 22-55, K8s-K5s, A2s/A3s/A6s/A7s, Q8s/J8s/T7s/86s/97s, offsuit broadways KJo/KTo/QJo/QTo/JTo, A9o (small mix), ATo (near-pure). Offsuit wheel/gapper aces A8o and below are folds at HJ. Approximate study chart, not exact solver output.

title: FR CO RFI
notes: CO in 9-max FR opens ~22-24% to 2.3bb; all-or-fold (callPct=0) since CO opens the pot. Core: pairs 55+ pure, with 33/22 mixed and 44 pure. All suited aces A2s+ are pure opens (A2s offers nut-flush/wheel equity). Suited broadways/kings down to K9s, QJs-Q9s, JTs-J9s, T9s-T8s pure; weaker suited gappers (K8s/K7s, Q8s, J8s, T7s, 97s, 86s, 75s) are mixed indifference points. Suited connectors 54s+ and 43s the bottom mixed defense. Offsuit is tight at CO: AT+/KJo/KQo/QJo pure, with KTo/QTo/JTo/A9o/T9o mixed and K9o/Q9o/J9o/98o pure fold. Vs tight tables lean to 24%; vs aggressive 3bettors behind trim mixed combos toward 22%.

title: FR BTN RFI
notes: FR 9-max BTN RFI is functionally identical to 6-max BTN: only SB and BB remain to act, so position dynamics, blind defense pressure, and optimal width match a 6-max button (~44-48%). Opener cannot call its own raise, so callPct=0 for every hand and foldPct=100-aggroPct. Full-frequency core: all 13 pairs, all suited aces (A2s+), all suited kings (K2s+), suited queens to Q6s, all the way down the suited-connector/one-gapper chain. The mixed-frequency boundary lives in: weak offsuit aces (A2o-A6o), the worst suited kings (K2s) and queens (Q3s-Q4s), offsuit broadway tails (K8o, Q8o, J8o, T8o, 97o, 87o, 76o), and suited gappers/baby connectors (J5s, T6s, 85s, 74s, 63s, 52s, 42s, 32s). Total combos ~601/1326 = ~45.4%, centered in the stated 44-48% band. Study-grade approximation aligned with published BTN solver charts, not exact solver output. Sizing 2.3bb is the modern small open.

title: FR SB RFI (raise-or-fold)
notes: SB vs BB only, raise-or-fold (no-limp) tree; callPct=0 by construction. In full GTO solutions the SB also employs a wide mixed-limp/completing strategy, but in this simplified raise-or-fold node the SB plays a linear ~40% raise range with 3bb sizing. All suited aces (A2s+) and suited kings down to ~K2s are in at high frequency for blocker/equity value; offsuit aces and kings taper monotonically. A5o carries a small wheel-blocker bump (70% vs A6o 65%) but is not raised above the stronger A7o/A8o. With only BB left to act, the open is very wide; thresholds are approximate and aligned to published charts, not exact solver output.

title: FR BB vs BTN open
rangePct: ~45% total defense (call + 3bet); 3bet ~6% of all hands = ~13-14% of the defending range (polar)
notes: BB closes the action vs a small ~2.3bb BTN open getting ~2.2:1, so it defends very wide and flats the bulk of its range. 3bet is polar to ~12bb: value = QQ+/AK (heavily) plus JJ/TT/AQs/KQs mixing; bluffs = suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s, A4s) and suited connectors/one-gappers at low frequency, with the very low Kxs/Qxs used only sparingly. Nearly all suited hands defend (most as pure or near-pure flats), and offsuit hands are call-or-fold with only the top offsuit broadways/Ax 3betting. Frequencies are illustrative mixes; round to the nearest pure action at the table (AA/KK = near-always 3bet with a small slowplay).

title: FR BB vs UTG
rangePct: ~6-7% (3bet, value-heavy/linear); plus a wide flat-call range (~18%) closing the action, ~24% total defense
notes: BB closes the action vs a tight UTG open getting ~4:1 (1bb already invested), so flatting is very wide. Total defense ~24% (3bet ~6.2% + call ~18%): appropriate for defending OOP into a strong nitty UTG range — far wider than the original's ~18.7% total, which was too tight. The 3bet is value-heavy/linear: QQ+/AK core, then a graded value layer JJ/TT/AQs/AQo/AJs/KQs/KJs etc. mostly as calls. Bluffs are kept light (~10% of 3bets) and come almost purely from suited wheel aces A5s-A2s (nut-flush + wheel equity + ace-blocker), which should NEVER appear with fold% — they are the bluff engine. Small/medium pairs set-mine as pure calls given the price. Offsuit Broadways and weak suited hands flat where playable and fold the rest. Study-grade approximations of published solver charts, not exact. Adjust toward more flatting/less 3betting if UTG is very tight, or open up if UTG is loose.

title: FR BB vs LJ
notes: Approximate study-grade range, NOT exact solver output. BB closes the action vs a single LJ (UTG) open at a great price, so the flatting range stays very wide (~10%): all pairs 22+, most suited hands, suited broadways, and many suited aces, plus offsuit broadways down to AJo/KQo. The 3bet (~7.5-8%) is value-weighted because LJ is the tightest open: QQ+/AK pure or near-pure 3bet for value, JJ/TT/AQ/KQs/AQo/AJs heavy 3bet, with the bluff group anchored by suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s) plus suited Broadway/connector backups (KQs/KJs/QJs/JTs, 87s-54s) that mix in. Realistically a solver BB-vs-UTG 3bet sits ~6-8%, so this is at the upper end of the stated ~9% target rather than literally 9%, to avoid forcing in junky bluffs.

title: FR BB vs HJ
sizing: 3bet to ~12bb (roughly 4x the HJ open, large OOP)
rangePct: ~9-10% 3bet, atop a wide ~15% flat-call range (~24% total defense)
notes: BB closes the action getting a price, so it defends very wide: a flat-call range near ~15% sits beneath a polarized ~9-10% 3bet. Value 3bets: QQ+, AKs/AKo, AQs, AQo (mostly), AJs/AJo and KQs (high freq), TT/JJ split toward 3bet. Bluff/semi-bluff 3bets favor blockers: wheel suited aces A5s-A2s (nut-flush playability + ace blocker, run at high freq) plus suited Broadway/connectors (KQs, KJs, QJs, JTs, T9s, 98s, 87s, 76s, 65s, 54s) at partial frequency. Offsuit A5o is a thin blocker 3bet/fold (rarely flat OOP). Vs a tight HJ, lean to the low end of the 3bet freq; vs a loose/exploitable HJ, call wider with suited gappers and small pairs rather than over-3betting. OOP, prefer 3betting marginal offsuit Broadways (KJo/QJo) over flatting to avoid tough postflop spots, while keeping suited equivalents in the flat range for set/flush/straight realization. ~12bb sizing (about 4x) is appropriate OOP to charge the wide field and reduce callers. Study-grade approximation, not exact solver output.

title: FR BB vs CO
notes: Approximate study-grade, NOT exact solver output. FR BB vs CO single-raised pot, BB closing action with excellent pot odds so the strategy is dominated by a very wide flat. 3bet is polarized: value = QQ+, AK, AQs, JJ/TT (mixed), AJs/KQs/AQo high-freq; bluffs = suited wheel aces (A5s/A4s/A3s lead the polar bluffs via blocker value), some Kxs (K5s) and suited connectors. Strong-but-dominated hands (TT-22, AJs, KQs, broadways, SCs) lean to flatting since BB is OOP and wants to keep CO's wide range in. Sizing ~12bb is large because BB is OOP and wants to deny equity vs a wide opener. Tighten bluffs / flat more vs a CO that 4bets aggressively; add more linear value 3bets vs a weak over-folding CO. Pure-fold offsuit junk and weak Qxs/Jxs/Txs offsuit omitted.

title: FR BTN vs CO
rangePct: ~9% (3bet portion, corrected from ~5.8% in original); plus a ~7-8% flatting range of pairs/broadways/suited connectors
notes: Approximate study-grade, solver-aligned (not exact). BTN vs CO is the widest single-raiser cold-call/3bet spot full-ring because Hero is IP with only the blinds left to act, so realization is excellent and a wide flat is justified. Corrected 3bet portion is now ~8.9% (combo-weighted), matching the stated ~9%; the original summed to only ~5.8%, too narrow for the claim. Structure: pure value = QQ+/AKs/AKo and near-pure AQs; merged value-3bets = JJ/TT/99 mixed, AQo/AJs/ATs/KQs/KJs/QJs/JTs/KTs/QTs at meaningful frequency; bluffs concentrated in suited wheel aces A5s-A2s (now the primary, properly weighted bluffs) plus suited blocker/connector mixes (K-high suited, suited gappers, mid-suited connectors). Flat range = pocket pairs, suited broadways/Ax-suited, suited connectors, and a thin slice of offsuit broadways (AJo/KQo/KTo/QTo) closing the action IP. Adjust toward more flatting vs tight/sticky CO opens, and toward a more polar high-frequency 3bet vs a CO that overfolds. Squeeze/multiway and ICM not modeled; tighten bluff-3bets vs short stacks in true tournament play.

title: FR CO vs LJ
notes: CO vs a tight LJ EP open with BTN/SB/BB behind. Key correction: CO is NOT closing the action and is OOP to BTN, so wide cold-calling invites squeezes. Value core QQ+/AKs/AKo 3-bets pure; JJ near-pure; TT/AQs heavily 3-bet rather than flatted because cold-calling premiums OOP with 4 players behind is exploitable to squeezes. Bluffs led by nut-blocker suited aces (A5s/A4s strongest, A3s/A2s lighter) plus suited broadways/connectors (KQs, AJs, KJs, JTs, QJs) that retain equity and unblock LJ folds. Offsuit broadways (AQo/KQo/AJo) mostly fold with a thin 3-bet bluff and small flat; flatting them OOP into squeeze range is a leak. Suited connectors (76s/87s/65s) are mostly fold with a small mixed 3-bet/flat. If squeeze-heavy table or LJ over-folds, raise A3s/A2s/65s 3-bet freq and trim flats; if LJ is a station, drop thin suited-ace bluffs and 3-bet a linear value range (more flats with KJs/QJs/89-type instead).

title: FR SB vs BTN
notes: SB vs BTN with no flat option (3bet-or-fold): SB is OOP and the BTN open is wide, so calling to play a bloated, blind-squeeze-prone pot OOP is dominated. Strategy is polar: value (TT+, AJs+, AKo/AQo, KQs/KJs) plus suited-wheel-ace and suited-broadway/connector bluffs (A5s-A2s, A4s/A3s, KTs/K5s/K4s, suited gappers, suited connectors down to 54s). Suited wheel aces (A5s>A4s>A3s>A2s) are 3bet at higher frequency than offsuit/middling aces because the ace blocker plus low-card blocker and nut-flush/straight equity make them premier bluffs. Mixed frequencies represent indifference at the bottom edge; round to pure 3bet vs pure fold at the table. Sizing ~11bb (~4-4.5x the ~2.5bb BTN open) leverages range and board advantage to offset positional disadvantage. Vs a tight or short BTN, cut the bluffs; vs a loose BTN, widen. Study-grade approximation aligned with published solver charts, not exact solver output.

title: FR BTN vs SB 3bet
notes: BTN vs SB 3bet, IP. 4bet is small/value-heavy at FR with 4bet-to-~22bb. Value core: AA/KK pure, QQ ~90% 4bet, AKs near-pure 4bet, AKo majority 4bet, with JJ/TT/AQs/AQo mostly flatting IP. Bluff/blocker representation = wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s/A3s) which block AA/AK/AQ and carry nut-flush playability; these primarily 4bet, secondarily flat — they do not fold much. Thin 4bet mixes from AJs/KQs/KJs round out bluffs. Because position is strong, BTN flats a wide range vs the 3bet (JJ-TT, AQs/AJs, KQs/KJs, AQo, suited broadways and pairs) rather than 4betting them; the calling range is much wider than the hands shown here. Study-grade approximations, not exact solver outputs.

title: FR CO vs BTN 3bet
notes: Approximate study-grade range, not solver-exact. CO is the OOP caller vs a BTN (IP) 3bet, so it has a disciplined, value-heavy 4bet (~6-7% of its open) plus a thin suited-wheel-ace bluff region, and a tighter calling range than an IP defender would have. Value 4bets: AA/KK at high freq, QQ as a 50/50 value/flat, AKs/AKo mostly mixed (flatting AK OOP keeps it in the calling range and avoids over-4betting). JJ-TT/AQ/AJ/KQ are predominantly flats or folds, not value 4bets, OOP. Bluff 4bets are suited wheel aces (A5s strongest, then A4s) for the ace blocker plus backdoor/wheel playability; these are essentially 4bet-or-fold with only a small flat. AQo and weaker offsuit broadways are dominated OOP and mostly fold. Calling OOP is less attractive than IP, so the flat region is narrower (no loose KJs/QJs/JTs heavy calls). Treat all splits as mixed strategies rounded to summary frequencies.

title: MTT HJ RFI
notes: Approximate study-grade HJ RFI, NOT solver-exact. Corrected width is ~22.6% (~300 combos / 1326), matching the stated ~22%. All pairs 44+ pure open; 33 mixes ~75%, 22 ~55%. All suited aces A2s+ pure open. Suited broadways and one-gappers down through QTs/JTs/T9s pure. Offsuit holdings are tight: AJo+/KQo/KJo/QJo pure, ATo pure, with KTo/QTo/JTo/T9o and the A9o/A5o wheel/blocker hands as a low-frequency tail. Suited connectors 87s/76s/65s/54s and 98s strong; weak suited gappers (K7s, Q8s, J8s, 86s, 75s, 64s) are low-frequency and region/solver dependent. callPct=0 throughout (RFI = open or fold). 2.1bb sizing. Treat as heuristics, not exact GTO.

title: MTT UTG RFI
notes: Open-or-fold spot: callPct=0 across the board, all hands sum to 100. Antes shift open odds favorably, so the range runs ~19% (a touch wider than 100bb cash UTG ~15%, within the stated 17-19% band). Core: all pairs 22+ (small pairs mixed), suited aces A2s-AKs (low wheel aces mixed/reduced, NOT the top of the ace family), suited Broadways, and suited connectors/gappers down to 54s. Offsuit is tighter: AKo-ATo, KQo-KTo, QJo at reduced frequency. Marginal hands are partial-open mixes reflecting solver indifference at this depth. At 40bb the 2.1bb open keeps risk-to-reward low; offsuit junk (K9o, Q9o, J9o, T9o) folds. NOT exact solver output.

title: MTT MP RFI
notes: MP (UTG+1 in 6-max) open-or-fold at 40bb with ante. Pure-fold hands omitted; callPct=0 throughout since this is an open-or-fold node (no limping). Total combos land around 21% of hands. Core is all pairs (small pairs partially mixed at the very bottom), all suited Broadways and suited Aces (A2s-A5s wheel cards retained for blocker/equity value), suited Kings down to K7s, suited connectors and the better one-gappers. Offsuit holdings are appropriately tight for MP: AKo-ATo, KQo-KTo, QJo-QTo, JTo only, mostly at reduced frequency at the bottom. The ante improves open EV and pushes the range slightly wider than a non-ante 40bb chart. Frequencies are study-grade approximations aligned with published solver charts, not exact solver output.

title: MTT CO RFI
notes: Approximate study-grade CO open for 40bb MTT with ante (NOT exact solver output). Ante presence widens the opening range vs non-ante and justifies the small 2.1bb sizing. Corrected range is ~28.5% of combos, within the stated ~27-29% band. callPct=0 throughout: this is an unopened first-in spot with no calling option. All pairs open; 44 now pure, 33/22 at reduced frequency as the bottom-pair tail. All suited aces open (A2s near-pure). The offsuit-ace blocker block is now a contiguous declining run A9o>A8o>A7o>A6o with A5o as a wheel/blocker mix, instead of the draft's hole (A7o/A6o were missing while A8o sat below a too-low A5o). Suited Broadways and connectors form the bulk; mixed-frequency hands (K6s, K5s, Q7s, J7s, T7s, 96s, 85s, 64s, 53s, 43s) sit at the margins. Offsuit holdings tighten fast past ATo/KJo/QJo/JTo. Tighten vs aggressive 3-bettors in the blinds or on a short/medium stack near a pay-jump/final-table dynamic.

title: MTT BTN RFI
notes: MTT 6-max 40bb with ante, BTN RFI. With antes the BTN open is the single widest RFI spot in MTT play; a ~47% range raise-or-folds nearly all suited hands plus all offsuit broadways, a wide band of offsuit Ax/Kx, and the better offsuit connectors. As an open spot callPct=0 throughout. Pure hands are 100% raise; marginal hands at the bottom of each cluster (low suited Kx/Qx/Jx, suited one/two-gappers, offsuit Ax wheel/low, offsuit gapper connectors) carry mixed frequencies reflecting solver indifference. A single 2.1bb size covers the whole range; limp/shove branches are negligible at 40bb.

title: MTT SB RFI (raise-or-fold)
notes: Raise-or-fold SB strategy, callPct=0 throughout (SB cannot profitably flat vs BB's positional/closing advantage, so the off-tree node collapses to open-or-fold). Corrected total open frequency ~43.5% (centered in the 42-46% band). In true MTT GTO at 40bb ante the SB node also mixes an open-limp range (polar: some trash suited gappers plus slowplayed premiums) and, at shorter effective stacks, occasional min-jams; this chart abstracts those into raise-first. Sizing 2.5bb is standard; smaller ~2.2-2.3bb or a 3bb-with-limp construction are alternative solver setups. Suited hands are far wider than offsuit due to BB's wide defense and worse realization for offsuit. Wheel aces (A5o/A4o/A5s-A2s) are kept at elevated frequency for blocker/equity value over weaker non-wheel Ax offsuit. Borderline mixed combos (K8o, K6o, Q8o, J6s/J4s, T5s, 95s, 84s, 63s, 52s, 42s, 32s) are coinflip-frequency and may be folded as a simplification with negligible EV loss.

title: MTT BB vs UTG
rangePct: ~9% 3bet (of the time vs the open); BB defends very wide overall (~25% of all hands continue, the large majority by calling)
notes: BB closes the action with a great price (UTG open + dead ante money), so the headline strategy is to defend a very wide range — the large majority by CALLING — with a tighter, value-leaning 3bet. The original range omitted almost the entire wide call-defense, which is the whole point of the spot. 3bet value core: AA-TT, AK, AQs/AJs at descending freq, KQs. Bluffs are dominated suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s, now pure-defend with NO preflop fold), plus low-freq suited Kx (K5s/K6s) and suited connectors for blockers/playability. Offsuit hands and weak-broadway/gapper hands are call-or-fold only. Sizing ~7.5bb is intentionally large because we are OOP and want to deny equity realization. Frequencies are approximate study-grade, not exact solver output.

title: MTT BB vs CO
notes: Approximate study-grade range aligned to published 40bb+ante MTT solver charts - NOT exact solver output. BB closes the action with excellent ante pot odds, so it flats extremely wide and 3bets a merged/linear ~12% construction. Value (QQ+/AK, near-pure; AQs/AJs/KQs/TT-99 heavy) drives the range; designated bluffs are the suited wheel aces A5s-A2s (best blockers/equity-when-called) plus suited connectors/one-gappers (54s-T9s) and a thin offsuit-ace leverage group (A5o/A4o). A6s-A9s stay near-pure flat - they are weak 3bet bluffs (poor blockers, dominated when called), so they should NOT exceed the wheel aces. Use ~8bb OOP to deny realization and build the pot with value. Frequencies are mixed; simplify at the table toward ~60/40 value/bluff. Flat wider vs tight openers; tighten/polarize more vs CO 4bet-happy opponents.

title: MTT BB vs BTN
sizing: 3bet to ~8bb (roughly 3.2x the BTN open OOP); pot-control flats keep the rest of the range
rangePct: ~14% 3bet (polar) atop a very wide ~23% pure flat-call range; ~37% of hands played total
notes: Polar 3bet vs BTN open at 40bb+ante: value core 3bets near-pure (QQ+, AKs, AKo, AQs, JJ, plus TT/99 leaning), and a heavy suited-blocker bluff load (A5s-A2s wheel aces, A7s-A6s, suited Broadways KTs/QJs/JTs, suited connectors/gappers 76s-43s, low Kx-suited K5s-K2s). Because BB closes the action OOP and realizes equity, the flat-call range stays extremely wide: every pair, every suited ace, almost all suited Broadways and connectors, and offsuit Broadways flat at high frequency. The fold region is offsuit trash, weak offsuit Kx/Qx/Jx, and the bottom of dominated suited gappers. Mixed study-grade frequencies; shift toward more bluff 3bets vs a BTN that overfolds and toward more flatting vs a station who calls light.

title: MTT BTN vs CO
notes: BTN vs CO is the widest IP flat/3bet spot. Premiums (AA-QQ, AKs/o, AQs) are pure/near-pure 3bets; value blends with flats (JJ/TT, AJs, KQs/KJs) since flatting IP is strong with dead ante money. Bluff 3bets favor blockers: wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s peak ~50-55, A6s ~35, tapering to A2s ~30) plus suited Broadways/connectors (KJs, QJs, T9s, 87s). Corrected 3bet width ~9.1% vs the stated ~10% target; VPIP ~23%. Offsuit Broadways (AQo, AJo, KQo, KJo) mix 3bet/flat; offsuit aces below ATo are mostly fold with a small blocker-bluff frequency. Sizing ~6.5bb is small/IP-appropriate at 40bb and does not commit, so offsuit bluffs stay trimmed. Approximate study-grade, not solver-exact.

title: MTT SB vs BTN
notes: SB vs BTN at 40bb +ante is a polar 3bet-or-fold spot OOP (no flatting), built from two clusters: (1) value/premiums for stacks-in equity (TT+ pure, AQs+/AK pure, AJs/ATs/KQs/KJs/KTs/QJs/QTs/JTs strong broadways, 99 mostly value), and (2) blocker/bluff hands that block BTN's continuing range and retain some flop equity. Wheel aces (A5s 90, A4s 85, A3s 75, A2s 55) are the best bluffs because they block AA/AK/AQ and have nut-straight/flush potential, so they are weighted higher than the A6s-A9s blockers (40-45%) which block fewer premiums. Suited kings K5s-K9s appear as light blocker bluffs (block KK/AK). Suited connectors (54s-98s, 76s, 87s) and offsuit broadways/blockers (A5o, A4o, KJo, KTo, QJo, QTo, JTo) round out the bluff region at lower frequency. callPct is 0 everywhere by design. With dead ante money the ~9bb (3x+) sizing buys fold equity and denies realization OOP. Approximate study-grade, not exact solver output.

title: MTT BTN vs SB 3bet
notes: At 40bb effective +ante, BTN plays a 4bet-or-fold-leaning strategy vs SB's 3bet, supplemented by IP flats. Stacks are shallow enough that 4bet-to-~18bb commits a meaningful portion, so value 4bets are heavy: AA/KK pure, QQ near-pure (95), AKs mostly, JJ/AKo mixed. Bluff 4bets concentrate on suited wheel aces (A5s/A4s as primary nibbles, A3s/A2s lighter) for ace blockers plus nut-flush/wheel playability when called. Strong broadways and middle pairs (AQ, AJs, ATs, KQs, TT-77) prefer flatting IP to keep SB's bluffs in and realize equity with position. Below ~77 and weak suited connectors are mostly folds or thin calls. Total 4bet frequency ~9%. Adjust toward more flats vs tight/value-heavy SB 3bet ranges, and toward more 4bet-bluffs vs a 3bet-happy SB. Ante slightly favors continuance — do not over-fold.

title: MTT CO vs BTN 3bet
sizing: 4bet to ~17bb (~0.42 pot, heavily committed; call jams with value tier)
rangePct: ~7% of CO continuing range (value-heavy, polarized; ~26 combos)
notes: OOP at 40bb the CO 4bet to ~17bb is heavily committed, so it is tight, value-dense and polarized. Value core: AA/KK 4bet near-always; QQ ~50/50 (frequent flat to avoid being jammed on while dominated by AK calls); AKs majority, AKo split (AKo does not want to bloat a commit OOP vs a positioned range). The light/bluff 4bets are suited wheel-aces (A5s primary, A4s, A3s sparingly) that block BTN AA/AKs/AKo value and retain backup equity; KQs a tiny blocker mix (blocks KK/QQ/AK). JJ/TT and the broadways (AQs/AQo/AJs/KJs/QJs) are predominantly flat-call or fold — they have no nut/blocker case to 4bet OOP and are dominated when called. Be ready to call a shove with the value tier (AA-QQ, AKs, often AKo) and fold the wheel-ace bluffs to the jam. Reduce bluff frequency vs nitty BTN 3bets; widen value-call thresholds vs loose 3bettors.
